highKEV: CVE-2007-0671 affects installed OpenOffice.app 4.1.11supply_chain
MITRE T1190

Installed software 'OpenOffice.app' (version '4.1.11') matches CISA KEV entry CVE-2007-0671 (Microsoft Office Excel contains a remote code execution vulnerability that can be exploited when a specially crafted Excel file is opened. This malicious file could be delivered as an email attachment or hosted on a malicious website. An attacker could leverage this vulnerability by creating a specially crafted Excel file, which, when opened, allowing an attacker to execute remote code on the affected system.). Verify whether your installed version is in the affected range; CISA KEV entries are actively exploited.

Evidence

{
  "installed": {
    "name": "OpenOffice.app",
    "version": "4.1.11"
  },
  "kev": {
    "cve": "CVE-2007-0671",
    "date_added": "2025-08-12",
    "due_date": "2025-09-02",
    "known_ransomware": "Unknown",
    "notes": "https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security-updates/securitybulletins/2007/ms07-015 ; https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2007-0671",
    "product": "Office",
    "summary": "Microsoft Office Excel contains a remote code execution vulnerability that can be exploited when a specially crafted Excel file is opened. This malicious file could be delivered as an email attachment or hosted on a malicious website. An attacker could leverage this vulnerability by creating a specially crafted Excel file, which, when opened, allowing an attacker to execute remote code on the affected system.",
    "vendor": "Microsoft",
    "vulnerability": "Microsoft Office Excel Remote Code Execution Vulnerability"
  }
}

Some of the impacted product(s) could be end-of-life (EoL) and/or end-of-service (EoS). Users are advised to discontinue use and/or transition to a supported version.",
    "vendor": "Microsoft",
    "vulnerability": "Microsoft Office Security Feature Bypass Vulnerability"
  }
}
highKEV: CVE-2026-21514 affects installed OpenOffice.app 4.1.11supply_chain
MITRE T1190

Installed software 'OpenOffice.app' (version '4.1.11') matches CISA KEV entry CVE-2026-21514 (Microsoft Office Word contains a reliance on untrusted inputs in a security decision vulnerability that could allow an authorized attacker to elevate privileges locally.). Verify whether your installed version is in the affected range; CISA KEV entries are actively exploited.

Evidence

{
  "installed": {
    "name": "OpenOffice.app",
    "version": "4.1.11"
  },
  "kev": {
    "cve": "CVE-2026-21514",
    "date_added": "2026-02-10",
    "due_date": "2026-03-03",
    "known_ransomware": "Unknown",
    "notes": "https://msrc.microsoft.com/update-guide/vulnerability/CVE-2026-21514 ; https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-21514",
    "product": "Office",
    "summary": "Microsoft Office Word contains a reliance on untrusted inputs in a security decision vulnerability that could allow an authorized attacker to elevate privileges locally.",
    "vendor": "Microsoft",
    "vulnerability": "Microsoft Office Word Reliance on Untrusted Inputs in a Security Decision Vulnerability"
  }
}
highPersistence entry references /tmp/persistence_outlier
MITRE T1543.001

Persistence artifact launchd:/System/Library/LaunchDaemons/com.apple.kdumpd.plist (collector macos.launchd) references a writable/world-shared path containing '/tmp/'. Programs in persistence points should live in protected system or signed app directories, not user-writable scratch space.

Evidence

{
  "match": "/tmp/",
  "subject": "launchd:/System/Library/LaunchDaemons/com.apple.kdumpd.plist"
}

highRWX region(s) in pid 11105 (plugin-container)memory_anomaly
MITRE T1055

Process plugin-container (pid 11105) holds 1 region(s) that are simultaneously readable, writable, and executable. Some legitimate JIT runtimes do this — but a plain native process with RWX pages is strongly suggestive of code injection.

Evidence

{
  "count": 1,
  "name": "plugin-container",
  "pid": 11105,
  "sample": [
    {
      "backing": "[anon]",
      "end": "0x3339a7d20000",
      "executable": true,
      "is_anonymous_exec": true,
      "is_backing_in_drop": false,
      "is_rwx": true,
      "offset": 0,
      "perms": "rwxp",
      "pid": 11105,
      "private": true,
      "readable": true,
      "size": 2143289344,
      "start": "0x333928120000",
      "writable": true
    }
  ]
}

highRWX region(s) in pid 95244 (plugin-container)memory_anomaly
MITRE T1055

Process plugin-container (pid 95244) holds 1 region(s) that are simultaneously readable, writable, and executable. Some legitimate JIT runtimes do this — but a plain native process with RWX pages is strongly suggestive of code injection.
